zh.lifeSummary: 當Lord John William Wrighte於1220年出生在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾林肯時，他的父親Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln29歲，母親Alice de l'Aigle25歲。他和Mrs. John William Wright至少有2個兒子。他去世於1265年在英國英格蘭艾可納享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lord John William Wrighte于1220年出生在英国英格兰林可爾史爾林肯时，他的父亲Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln29岁，母亲Alice de l'Aigle25岁。他和Mrs. John William Wright至少有2个儿子。他去世于1265年在英国英格兰艾可納享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Memoli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Ciro, Francesco, Luciano, Natale, Nino.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a pet form of thepersonal name Guglielmo, Italian form of William .

variants.name: McElligott
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brigid, Brendan, Seamus.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uileagóid, a patronymic from a pet form of the Gaelic form of the personal name William .

variants.name: McWilliam
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scottish Iain, Morag.

Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , also Anglicized as Williamson in Scotland. The name is thought to be mainly Scottish in Ulster, though there has been some confusion with McQuillan in County Down. In Connacht, McWilliam was a branch of Burke .

variants.name: Wilcock
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Wilcoc, a pet form of William (composed of the elements Will 1 + the hypocoristic suffix -cok; see Cocke ).

variants.name: McWilliams
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Uilleim, a patronymic from a Gaelicized form of William , with excrescent English -s. In Ireland it sometimes represents a partial Gaelicization of Welsh Williams .

variants.name: Lemmo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Donato, Gino.
Italian (southern): from the personal name Lemmo, a pet form of Guglielmo, an equivalent of William . Compare Lembo .

variants.name: Wilk
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Mariusz, Stanislaw, Tadeusz, Zygmunt, Andrzej, Bogdan, Boguslaw, Casimir, Krzysztof, Stanislawa, Boleslaw, Darek.

Polish: from the vocabulary word wilk ‘wolf’, probably applied as a nickname for someone thought to resemble a wolf or connected with wolves in some way. Alternatively, from an Old Slavic personal name based on vlъkъ ‘wolf’.
English (Staffordshire): from Middle English Wilke, a pet form of William and probably a shortening of Wilkin . The usage was very likely Flemish in origin.

variants.name: Wilkin
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Wilkin, a pet form of William introduced to England and Scotland by Flemings and Picards after the Norman Conquest.
North German: variant of   Wilken  .

variants.name: Gillam
meanings: English: variant of   Gilliam  .
Welsh: Anglicized form of   Gwilliam  .

variants.name: Lembo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Carmelo, Domenic, Pietro, Alfio, Amelio, Antonio, Biagio, Dante, Giovani, Giovanni.
Italian (southern): from a hypercorrected form of Lemmo, a pet form of Guglièlmo, an equivalent of William .

nameMeanings.name: Wrighte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Wright  .

variants.name: Wright
meanings: English and Scottish: occupational name for a craftsman or maker of machinery, mostly in wood, of any of a wide range of kinds, from Middle English and Older Scots wriht, wright, wricht, writh, write (Old English wyrhta, wryhta) ‘craftsman’, especially ‘carpenter, joiner’. The term is found in various combinations (for example, Cartwright and Wainwright ), but when used in isolation it often referred to a builder of windmills or watermills. This surname is also very common among African Americans.

nameMeanings.name: Lord
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably a respelling of   Laura  , that coincides in form with the German spelling of the same name and also with the medieval English given name.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When Lord John William Wrighte was born in 1220, in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln, was 29 and his mother, Alice de l'Aigle, was 25. He had at least 2 sons with Mrs. John William Wright. He died in 1265, in Nazeing, Essex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lord John William Wrighte est né en 1220, à Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Alice de l'Aigle, avait 25 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Mrs. John William Wright. Il est décédé en 1265, à Nazeing, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 45 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lord John William Wrighte 1220 in Lincoln, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln 29 und seine Mutter Alice de l'Aigle 25. Er hatte mit Mrs. John William Wright mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1265 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Nazeing, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lord John William Wrighte è nato il nel 1220 nel Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln, aveva 29 anni e il suo madre, Alice de l'Aigle, aveva 25 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Mrs. John William Wright. Morì nel 1265, nel Nazeing, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 45 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lord John William Wrighte(이)가 1220년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 링코컨에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Alice de l'Aigle(은)는 25세였다. 그는 Mrs. John William Wright(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1265년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 나즈인그에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lord John William Wrighte nasceu em 1220, em Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Alice de l'Aigle, tinha 25 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Mrs. John William Wright. Ele faleceu em 1265, em Nazeing, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 45 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lord John William Wrighte родился в 1220 г. в местности линколн, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice de l'Aigle, 25. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Mrs. John William Wright. Он умер в 1265 г. в местности назинг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 45 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lord John William Wrighte nació en 1220, en Lincoln, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John de Lacy 2nd Earl of Lincoln, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Alice de l'Aigle, tenía 25 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Mrs. John William Wright. Murió en 1265, en Nazeing, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 45 años.
